key_points: The poem describes a mysterious and 'funny' little man who is as quiet as a mouse
He is the one responsible for all the small mischief and accidents in every house
No one has ever actually seen his face, yet everyone knows he exists
The poem uses this character to explain how things get broken or misplaced without anyone taking the blame
It is a lighthearted way of looking at the little mistakes we make every day
examples: When a plate is cracked, the poem says the funny man did it
He is blamed for leaving the door ajar or losing a button
He is compared to a 'quiet mouse' because no one hears him doing his mischief
The 'funny man' is like a ghost that lives in everyone's home
Even though he is 'funny,' he is also a bit of a troublemaker
formulas_or_processes: Mischief → A plate is broken or a mess is made
Reaction → Everyone denies doing it ('It wasn't me!')
Explanation → Blaming the 'Funny Man' who no one can see
Result → The mystery remains unsolved, but the blame is shifted
exam_tips: Remember that the funny man is a symbol for the mistakes we don't want to admit to
Focus on the comparison: he is as 'quiet as a mouse'
Note that 'no one has ever seen his face'—this is a key detail in the poem
The main mood of the poem is humorous and playful
